title: German Pork Roast
description: A classic German-style pork roast with a delicate crust, perfect for a special occasion.
instructions: Season the pork loin with salt, black pepper, and thyme.
Heat a large oven-safe pan over medium-high heat.
Sear the pork loin on all sides until browned, about 2-3 minutes per side.
Transfer the pan to the preheated oven and roast for 20 minutes.
Reduce the oven temperature to 180°C (350°F) and continue roasting for an additional 20-25 minutes, or until the internal temperature reaches 63°C (145°F).
While the pork is roasting, cook the bacon in a separate pan until crispy.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the chopped carrots and cook for an additional 5 minutes.
Add the beer and brown sugar to the pan, stirring to combine.
Simmer the sauce over low heat until the pork is done.
Let the pork rest for 10 minutes before slicing and serving with the roasted vegetables and sauce.
Serve with boiled potatoes or roasted potatoes, if desired.
